faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What is a Vijaysar wooden glass?
faq.a1: A Vijaysar wooden glass is a traditional Indian tumbler handcrafted from Vijaysar wood (Pterocarpus marsupium), also known as the Indian Kino tree. Vijaysar wood has been used in Ayurvedic wellness traditions for centuries. Water stored overnight takes on a slight natural tinge from the wood — this is completely normal and safe. It is used as part of a daily wellness and hydration ritual.
faq.q2: How do I use the Vijaysar tumbler?
faq.a2: (1) Fill the tumbler with room temperature drinking water. (2) Cover and keep overnight for 6–8 hours. (3) Drink the Vijaysar wood infused water first thing in the morning on an empty stomach. (4) Rinse gently with plain water, dry thoroughly, and refill for the next day. Repeat daily.
faq.a3: Yes! Cash on Delivery is available all across India. You pay in cash when the product is delivered. No advance payment required for COD orders.
faq.a4: Yes! Free delivery on all orders across India — Pack of 1, 2, or 5. No minimum order value.
faq.q5: How many days does delivery take?
faq.a5: Orders dispatched within 1–2 business days. Metro cities: 2–4 days. Other cities: 3–6 days. Remote areas: 5–8 days.
faq.q6: What is the return and replacement policy?
faq.a6: 7-day replacement/return policy from the date of delivery. If your product arrives damaged or defective, contact us within 7 days and we will arrange a replacement.
faq.q7: How do I clean the Vijaysar glass?
faq.a7: Rinse gently with plain water only. No soap, detergent, or harsh chemicals — these damage the natural wood. Do not use a dishwasher. Dry completely after rinsing and store in a dry, ventilated place.
faq.a8: No. The Vijaysar wooden glass is NOT a medicine and does NOT cure diabetes or any disease. It is a traditional Indian wellness product used as part of a healthy hydration routine. People with diabetes or any medical condition must consult their doctor before making changes to their routine.

problem.p1.title: Too Much Sugar in Daily Diet
problem.p1.body: Sweets, chai, processed foods — sugar sneaks into every meal. You want to be more mindful but don't know where to anchor a healthy habit.
problem.p2.title: No Healthy Morning Routine
problem.p2.body: You wake up, reach for your phone, have chai, and rush. There's no simple, grounding wellness ritual to start your day right.
problem.p3.title: Searching for Natural Alternatives
problem.p3.body: Expensive supplements and pills feel artificial. You want something rooted in Indian tradition — natural, simple, and trustworthy.
problem.p4.title: Worried About Parents' Health
problem.p4.body: You want to gift your parents something meaningful — not another pill, but a timeless natural daily habit they'll actually love.
problem.p5.title: No Time for Complex Routines
problem.p5.body: Complex diets don't stick. You need something that takes 30 seconds each morning and builds into a habit automatically.
problem.p6.title: Disconnected from Ayurvedic Roots
problem.p6.body: Our grandparents had simple, effective wellness traditions. In the rush of modern life, those traditions have been forgotten.

steps.s1.title: Step 1 — Fill
steps.s1.body: Fill the tumbler with normal room temperature drinking water. Do not use hot water.
steps.s2.title: Step 2 — Rest
steps.s2.body: Cover and keep overnight for 6–8 hours. Let the Vijaysar wood naturally infuse into the water.
steps.s3.title: Step 3 — Drink
steps.s3.body: First thing in the morning, drink the Vijaysar wood infused water — ideally on an empty stomach.
steps.s4.title: Step 4 — Rinse & Repeat
steps.s4.body: Rinse gently with plain water. Dry thoroughly. Refill tonight. Build your daily ritual.
care.heading: 📋 Care Tips
care.tip1: Use only room temperature water — never hot
care.tip2: Rinse with plain water only — no soap or chemicals
care.tip4: Do not soak for more than 8–10 hours

faq.a9: Yes! We accept all major payment methods via Razorpay — UPI (GPay, PhonePe, Paytm), debit cards, credit cards, net banking, and wallets.
faq.q10: How long should water be kept in the Vijaysar glass?
faq.a10: Ideally 6–8 hours. An overnight soak is the most convenient option — fill it before bed and your infused water is ready each morning. Do not soak for more than 10 hours.
faq.a11: No. Use only room temperature or cold drinking water. Hot water can damage the natural wood. Never pour boiling or warm water into the tumbler.
faq.q12: Is Vijaysar glass safe for people with diabetes?
faq.a12: The Vijaysar wooden glass is a traditional Ayurvedic wellness product — it is NOT a medicine and does NOT treat, cure, or prevent diabetes or any other medical condition. Many people with diabetes use it as part of a healthy daily hydration habit, but it should not replace prescribed medication or medical advice. Always consult your doctor before making changes to your health routine.
faq.a13: Absolutely! It makes a beautiful, meaningful gift — especially for parents, in-laws, and elders. The Family Pack of 5 is our most popular gifting option. We can deliver directly to your recipient's address.
faq.q14: Does the wood colour vary between glasses?
faq.a14: Yes. Since each glass is made from natural wood, grain pattern, colour, and texture will vary slightly between pieces. This is not a defect — it is a natural characteristic of handcrafted wooden products.
faq.q15: Is this product suitable for daily use?
faq.a15: Yes. With proper care — rinsing with plain water, drying thoroughly, and storing in a dry place — it will last a long time.
faq.q16: How many days should I use the Vijaysar glass continuously?
faq.a16: Traditional Ayurvedic practice recommends using the Vijaysar glass daily for 90 days for a complete wellness cycle, then taking a 15–30 day break before resuming. Most people notice a subtle change in their water taste within the first few days. Consistent daily use is key.
faq.q17: Vijaysar glass vs copper glass — which is better?
faq.a17: They serve different purposes. Copper glasses are used primarily for their antimicrobial properties. Vijaysar wooden glasses are used specifically for the natural wood infusion. Many households keep both. If you are new to Ayurvedic rituals, the Vijaysar glass is gentler to start with.
faq.q18: Can children use the Vijaysar wooden glass?
faq.a18: Vijaysar wood is a natural material with no synthetic coatings or chemicals. However, it is intended for adults. We do not specifically recommend it for young children without consulting a paediatrician or Ayurvedic practitioner first. Teenagers and adults can use it as part of a healthy daily routine.
faq.q19: Why does the water turn pinkish or brownish overnight?
faq.a19: This is completely normal and expected — it is the natural tannins of the Vijaysar wood infusing into the water. The slight colour change (from pale pink to light brown) is a sign that the wood is authentic and active. The water is safe to drink. If your water shows no colour change at all after a week of use, contact us for a replacement.
faq.q20: Is Vijaysar glass better than plastic or steel bottles?
faq.a20: Yes, from a wellness perspective. Plastic bottles can leach microplastics and chemicals over time. Steel bottles are neutral. The Vijaysar wooden glass adds the natural wood infusion to water, making it part of an active Ayurvedic wellness ritual. It is not a water bottle for carrying — it is a dedicated daily-ritual tumbler.
faq.a21: Vijaysar, botanically known as Pterocarpus marsupium, is a large deciduous tree native to India and Sri Lanka, commonly called the Indian Kino Tree. Its heartwood has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries. It is mentioned in classical Ayurvedic texts including Charaka Samhita. Our glasses are handcrafted from the heartwood of mature Vijaysar trees sourced responsibly from licensed suppliers.
faq.q22: What are the side effects of Vijaysar wood?
faq.a22: Vijaysar wood (Pterocarpus marsupium) is considered safe for most healthy adults when used as directed — drinking the infused water once daily in the morning. No serious side effects are widely reported from traditional use. However, people who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or on prescribed medication (especially for diabetes or blood pressure) should consult their doctor before use. If you experience any unusual discomfort, discontinue use and consult a healthcare professional.
faq.q23: Can Vijaysar glass help with cholesterol management?
faq.a23: In Ayurvedic tradition, Vijaysar wood (Pterocarpus marsupium) is associated with supporting metabolic wellness, which includes healthy fat metabolism. It is used as part of a holistic daily routine — not as a treatment for cholesterol or any medical condition. The Vijaysar glass is a traditional wellness product and should complement, not replace, medical advice or prescribed medication. Always consult your doctor if you have concerns about cholesterol.
faq.q24: Is Vijaysar wood good for liver and digestive health?
faq.a24: Ayurvedic practitioners have traditionally used Vijaysar (Pterocarpus marsupium) as part of holistic wellness routines associated with digestive and metabolic health. The bitter and astringent properties (Tikta and Kashaya Rasa) of the wood are considered beneficial for Kapha dosha balance in Ayurveda. This is a traditional perspective — the Vijaysar glass is not a medicine and does not treat liver disease or digestive conditions. Consult your doctor for any medical concerns.
faq.q25: How long does a Vijaysar wooden glass last?
faq.a25: With proper care, a Vijaysar wooden glass typically lasts 12–18 months of daily use. Signs it is time to replace: no colour change in the water after overnight soaking, visible cracks, or persistent unusual smell. Rinse with plain water only, dry thoroughly after each use, and store in a dry place to maximise its lifespan. We offer a 7-day replacement guarantee from the date of delivery.
faq.q26: Can Vijaysar glass support weight management?
faq.a26: In Ayurvedic tradition, Vijaysar wood is associated with balancing Kapha dosha — the metabolic force connected to weight, fluid retention, and sluggish digestion. Drinking Vijaysar wood infused water as a morning ritual is a traditional practice for overall metabolic wellness. It is not a weight loss treatment. For sustainable weight management, the glass works best as part of a balanced diet and active lifestyle. It is not a substitute for medical advice.
